message:
fix(widgets/emoji): decouple fallback rendering from styling + respect Skeleton degradation

Fifth wave of the degradation-handling cleanup across
ftui-widgets.  Before this commit, Emoji::render conflated two
orthogonal concerns:

  - "Does this terminal support rendering unicode emoji glyphs?"
    (terminal capability)
  - "Is the current DegradationLevel allowing styling?"
    (render budget)

The old code used `deg.apply_styling()` as a proxy for "unicode
emoji are available", which was wrong in both directions:

  1. NoStyling degradation suppressed colors AND fell back to
     ASCII text, even on terminals that render emoji perfectly.
     Every fallback-bearing Emoji widget lost its actual emoji
     glyph whenever the render budget tightened.
  2. Full-styling degradation ALWAYS rendered the unicode emoji
     directly, even on dumb terminals without unicode support —
     the fallback path was unreachable outside the NoStyling
     branch.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/emoji.rs

- Import TerminalCapabilities from ftui_core so the widget can
  ask the real terminal capability layer whether unicode_emoji
  is supported, independently of the render budget.

- Widget::render() rewritten:

    1. Standard degradation snapshot + render_content()
       short-circuit (same pattern as the earlier
       NoStyling/Skeleton commits in this series).

    2. Decide `use_fallback` based on BOTH conditions:
         - the widget has a fallback string configured, AND
         - TerminalCapabilities::with_overrides().unicode_emoji
           is false.
       A terminal that supports emoji now always sees the real
       glyph, regardless of degradation level.

    3. Compute the (text, style) pair in one branch:
         - use_fallback → (fallback_text, fallback_style or
           Style::default() depending on apply_styling)
         - otherwise   → (self.text, self.style or
           Style::default() depending on apply_styling)

    4. Single draw_text_span call at the end.

  The matrix of behaviors is now:

    unicode_emoji=true,  apply_styling=true  → emoji + color
    unicode_emoji=true,  apply_styling=false → emoji + default
    unicode_emoji=false, apply_styling=true  → fallback + color
    unicode_emoji=false, apply_styling=false → fallback + default
    render_content=false (Skeleton)          → no-op

- New imports in the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block:
    ftui_core::capability_override::{CapabilityOverride, with_capability_override}
    ftui_render::budget::DegradationLevel

- New unit tests use with_capability_override() to drive all
  four matrix cells plus the Skeleton no-op case:
    - render_uses_emoji_when_terminal_supports_unicode_and_styling_enabled
    - render_uses_emoji_when_terminal_supports_unicode_with_no_styling
    - render_uses_fallback_when_terminal_lacks_unicode_emoji
    - render_uses_fallback_default_style_when_no_styling
    - render_skeleton_is_noop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
